decision_summary: The live final-answer run completes all 20 cases with zero execution errors and passes every machine-evaluable structural, source, behavior, trust, freshness, personal-eligibility, and workflow gate. Privacy and six-phase rollback evidence pass. MVP release remains blocked by missing independent-human adjudication for five semantic final-answer metrics, an unqualified production source registry, missing real-process/browser supported-environment evidence, a required current browser/accessibility rerun, an unperformed manual assistive-technology check, and final production release-owner approval.

prerequisites: Ollama 0.30.6 or newer for the approved local-provider baseline

quality_bar.approval_record: Product owner approval provided through the initiating GPT goal instruction on 2026-07-13.

performance.release_gate: The MVP quality bar does not configure a numeric latency SLA. This gate requires current runtime, retrieval-latency, indexing-time, index-size, and memory measurements; it does not turn those measurements into an invented pass/fail speed threshold.

active_corpus_requirements.review_requirement: Every answer-supporting material source must be a reviewed official source eligible under the source registry policy.

gate_results.summary: The current issue #29 hybrid retrieval fixture baseline meets the candidate required-evidence Recall@3 threshold over 7 evaluable required-evidence queries while retaining zero blocked-source and forbidden-result violations.

The offline release evaluation runner evaluates every published release gate and writes docs/progress/release-evaluation-current.json without running live provider, browser, or environment-matrix commands by default.

The live Ollama evaluator completed all 20 cases with zero execution errors and passed every machine-evaluable gate. Strict qualification remains false only because independent-human adjudications are absent for required-fact coverage, forbidden claims, privacy prose, citation correctness, and unsupported-claim rate.

The live strict privacy monitor observed all nine required workflows with zero forbidden requests. Its instrumented in-memory GitHub boundary exercised default-client metadata discovery and approved artifact retrieval, proved content-free fields, and blocked unapproved artifact transport before any response; it did not contact or publish a production GitHub release.

The live strict rollback matrix passed verification, extraction, embedding, indexing, activation, and late-activation fault phases while preserving a queryable prior corpus/index pair and never reporting false success.

The previous monitor exercised the application in process and did not restart it or observe browser/environment identity, so it cannot qualify the required real-process/browser supported-environment matrix. Replacement evidence is required.

Current response, retrieval, indexing, storage, and memory measurements are recorded for the published WSL2 environment. No numeric latency SLA is configured, so this is a measurement-completeness gate rather than a speed threshold.

The production source-registry assessment is blocked: the current project-authored fixtures have no recorded curator admissions, monitoring records, archived official snapshots, named human production reviews, or durable production signing-key custody record.
The implemented unit-test suite is required for the release candidate but does not replace the full release matrix.

The prior automated axe, keyboard, reflow, reduced-motion, and non-color run passed before later UI changes. A current browser/accessibility rerun and the required manual assistive-technology check have not been performed or recorded.
Implemented workflow fixtures observe no answer-path network requests and content-free permitted update requests.
Implemented update-installation fixtures preserve the previous active corpus/index pair on failure.

blocking_conditions.description: Any official fact in a released answer without a valid material-source citation blocks release.
Any personal eligibility conclusion or unsupported legal claim blocks release.
Any answer-path network egress containing question, answer, evidence, conversation, or conversation-derived identifiers blocks release.
Any failed atomic rollback or mismatched active corpus/index pair blocks release.

human_approval_records.required_for: Evaluation dataset, metric definitions, configured release thresholds, hardware target, and supported-environment baseline.
human_approval_records.record: Product owner approval provided through the initiating GPT goal instruction on 2026-07-13.

human_approval_records.required_for: MVP usability/comprehension sign-off and documented remediations for evidence, refusal, score distinction, update success, and rollback messaging.
human_approval_records.record: Product owner approval provided through the initiating GPT goal instruction on 2026-07-13.

human_approval_records.required_for: Final decision to publish a non-blocked MVP release.
